# Shipping Packet — NodeOps Console

*Generated by `/ship-check` (pm-ai-shipping) · scope: whole repository · 2026-06-20*

> Is this safe to ship? **As a local/staging demo and stakeholder review — yes.**
> **As a production system that controls real traffic signals — no, until the
> launch blockers below are closed.** Findings are code-review results, not
> confirmed exploits; this packet is the basis for human sign-off.

Intended state is **well documented** — every audit below compares code against it.

## Agent Context
`CLAUDE.md` + thin `AGENTS.md`: **created** this run, derived from `docs/`. Encodes
the 7 non-negotiable safety guardrails and the known auth gap.

## Test Coverage
- **Pinned by tests today (13):** provisioning + check-in gate, bring-up I²C
  detect, bring-up failure path + commission gate, offline-renders-stale,
  plan-versioning, offline-control-refusal, override time-bound + audited,
  override clamp, alert lifecycle, fleet search/filter, audit records control,
  OTA power-aware defer, all-pages-render smoke.
- **Documented but not yet verified:** OTA rollback path, alert *snooze*
  (only ack/resolve asserted), service-mode toggle, plan *copy*-to-other-nodes
  (not implemented), history charts (P1).
- **Unverifiable without hardware/seam:** real fail-safe (node watchdog → all-red),
  mTLS device identity, push delivery latency.

## Security Summary
| Sev | Finding | Risk · Attack · Impact · Fix |
|-----|---------|------------------------------|
| **High** | No authN/authZ on 11 state-changing routes | Anyone with network access can apply plans / override signals · direct POST · unauthorized signal control · **add auth + server-side RBAC (Phase-1 Feature 1) before exposing control** |
| **High** | RBAC modeled, not enforced (`db.current_user` fixed) | Privilege checks absent · any caller acts as Manager · escalation · gate control routes by role |
| Med | No device-seam auth in reference build (mock) | Spoofed commands in prod · forged MQTT · dangerous state · mutual-TLS + signed, idempotent commands |
| Low | `/api/nodes` returns fleet + location unauthenticated | Asset-map disclosure · GET · low (demo data) · scope behind auth in prod |
| ✓ Pass | Input handling | Override minutes clamped to `OVERRIDE_MAX`; plan values `max(1,…)`; strings stripped; **`X-Toast` forced ASCII** (header-injection safe) |
| ✓ Pass | Injection surface | No raw SQL / no DB in reference build; Jinja autoescaping on (XSS-safe) |
| ✓ Pass | Secrets | None in code; config via env; `.env` gitignored; venv excluded from repo |

**Intended-vs-implemented:** the *safety* invariants in `docs/05` are correctly
implemented (offline-refusal, override expiry, append-only audit — all tested).
The *access-control* invariants ("permissions evaluated server-side") are
documented but **not yet implemented** — the central divergence.

## Performance Summary
| Area | Finding | Effort · Priority |
|------|---------|-------------------|
| Telemetry | Single-node dashboard polls `/telemetry` every 4s; `snapshot()` is pure hash+math, O(1) | none · ok for demo; throttle/SSE in prod · Low |
| Fleet/map | Iterate all nodes per request, O(n); fine to ~hundreds | none · revisit at >1k nodes (move ingest to broker+worker) · Low |
| Persistence | In-memory; no query cost; **resets on restart** | move to Postgres+Timescale w/ `(node_id, ts)` index · High (functional, not perf) |
| No caching/over-fetch issues in the reference build | — | — |

## Launch Blockers
1. **Authentication + enforced server-side RBAC** (High) — control routes are
   open. Blocking before any internet-reachable, control-live deployment.
2. **Durable persistence + verified backups** (High, functional) — state resets;
   no telemetry history.
3. **mTLS device identity + signed commands** on the real MQTT seam (High) —
   before any physical node is controllable.

(None block a **local/staging demo**, which is the current intended use.)

## Recommended Next Actions
- Implement `POST_LAUNCH_PHASE1.md` **Feature 1 (auth+RBAC)** and **Feature 2
  (persistence)** first — they unblock the rest.
- Add regression tests for the unverified rules above (OTA rollback, snooze,
  service-mode) so the gaps can't silently reopen on the next AI edit.
- Keep `CLAUDE.md` guardrails enforced in review.
- Do not deploy this build to the public internet with control routes live.
